title: ophrys bilunulata subsp. sancti-isidorii
intro: Ophrys bilunulata subsp. sancti-isidorii, also known as the Santis Isidoro Bee Orchid, is a rare and endemic orchid species found in the Cantabrian Mountains of Spain. The plant is known for its unique, two-lobed lip that mimics the appearance of a female bee, attracting male bees for pollination. The medicinal properties of Ophrys bilunulata subsp. sancti-isidorii include its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. It is traditionally used in herbal preparations to promote digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, often prepared as a tea or tincture.
